completed_tasks.name: sLat sign-clamp: reset to SS when sLat opposes latVelCur
SLIP_EPSILON 0.1→3.0 and sLat SS overshoot cap
HUD vLong fix: store omegaCur-longVelCur instead of raw hub vel

remaining_tasks.name: Fix yaw oscillation root cause: sLong/wheel-omega positive feedback at rear wheels

blockers.description: BUG-03 yaw slosh still present despite sLat fixes. Root cause identified as sLong/wheel-omega positive feedback loop at rear wheels during yaw spin, not sLat spring energy.

decisions.rationale: Prevents vCon→0 at rest which let sLat_ss grow to several meters of spring energy
Prevents sLat from exceeding current-velocity steady state, eliminates sLat-driven springback
Resets sLat to SS when it has wrong-direction sign vs latVelCur

next_action: Investigate sLong/wheel-omega positive feedback at RL/RR wheels during yaw rotation. The feedback: wY→longVelCur_RL(from spin)→sLong negative→Flong backward at RL→T_yaw positive (RL is at r_x=-0.77m so backward Flong creates +yaw torque)→wY grows. Need to decouple yaw-rotation-induced hub longitudinal velocity from the sLong input, OR add yaw angular damping, OR apply an impulse clamp on T_yaw.
context_notes: Three logs analyzed. sLat at FL/FR is now capped correctly (fl_sa dropped from 0.5 to 0.04). But rl_sa and rr_sa are still 0.5-1.5m dominated by sLong from wheel omega oscillation. The rl_omega swings from +4.83 to -7.36 rad/s during a spin — this is the wheel spinning up/down from the yaw-induced longitudinal hub velocity. The Flong from this creates a yaw torque that amplifies the spin. Oscillation amplitude: wY decays from 2.0 to ~0.9 to ~0.6 per half-cycle — damping slowly.
